opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My mouth feels like it's stuffed full of burning cotton, and I can't even drink water without wincing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: White coating and burning in my mouth after antibiotics is stopping me eating and drinking.

symptom_narrative.onset: 3 days ago, just after finishing a 7-day course of antibiotics
symptom_narrative.character: cottony feeling, thick white curd-like coating, loss of taste, burning
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: cracking at the corners of the mouth; NO deep chest pain on swallowing, no difficulty getting food to the stomach, no fever

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recent chest infection treated with antibiotics

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; reduced intake from pain

objective_data_offered.value: thick white curd-like coating on tongue/cheeks, cracks at mouth corners, visible on video
no deep chest pain on swallowing; drinking painful due to mouth burning
